content: Which from following elements is a decay product of thorium?
explanation:
Thorium-232 undergoes a series of α- and β-decays called the “thorium series.” In this chain one of the members is polonium (Po).

Step-by-step (showing only key α-steps):

$$^{232}_{90}\mathrm{Th}\;\xrightarrow{\alpha}\;^{228}_{88}\mathrm{Ra}$$

$$^{228}_{88}\mathrm{Ra}\;\xrightarrow{\beta^-}\;^{228}_{89}\mathrm{Ac}\;\xrightarrow{\beta^-}\;^{228}_{90}\mathrm{Th}$$

$$^{228}_{90}\mathrm{Th}\;\xrightarrow{\alpha}\;^{224}_{88}\mathrm{Ra}$$

$$^{224}_{88}\mathrm{Ra}\;\xrightarrow{\alpha}\;^{220}_{86}\mathrm{Rn}$$

$$^{220}_{86}\mathrm{Rn}\;\xrightarrow{\alpha}\;^{216}_{84}\mathrm{Po}$$

Here, $$^{216}_{84}\mathrm{Po}$$ is a polonium isotope formed as a decay product of thorium.

None of the other options (S, Se, Te) appear in the thorium-232 decay chain.

Answer: Option C (Po).
